title: Spot the ISS
description: Use a satellite-tracking app on your smartphone and a basic telescope to watch the International Space Station pass overhead and log the time in your mission logbook.

dialogue.text: The ISS crosses the sky a few times each week. Use your smartphone's satellite-tracking app to plan tonight's pass, pick an open area away from traffic and bright lights, and keep the telescope capped indoors so it never points near the Sun.

Open a satellite-tracking app such as Heavens-Above on your smartphone, allow location access, and note the predicted time in your mission logbook. Set your basic telescope on a stable tripod, keep paths clear, and never aim it near the Sun or at people.

options.text: The app shows a flyover soon.

dialogue.text: Awesome! It's inspiring to see humans living in orbit. Cap the telescope, close the app, pack up your gear, and watch your footing in the dark.
